role_title: Product Manager, Trip Quality Merchandising and AI
secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with marketplace/trust domain focus

seniority.level: Senior IC / Principal PM (likely Staff/Principal level)
level_signals: Comp range $232K-$282K base, consistent with Staff/Principal PM at Airbnb
'Desire to do individual contributor product management work' signals senior IC not people manager
'Demonstrated track record of building cross-functional and executive leadership alignment'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Hands-on track record shipping AI/ML-powered features on consumer products — including generative AI features (LLM-based summarization, content generation) and ML ranking or personalization systems — with direct involvement in defining evaluation criteria and improving model outputs.
Expert ability to use data and business analysis to inform product strategy and drive decisions.
Experience creating product messaging and delivering to customers.
Demonstrated track record of building cross-functional and executive leadership alignment.
Experience working on consumer products with marketplace dynamics on a global scale.
Entrepreneurial track record of taking an idea to reality.
Excited to lead cross-functional execution, from strategy through shipped product in a fast-paced environment.
Desire to do individual contributor product management work.
Excellent written and verbal communication; adept at simplifying complex AI/ML and data concepts for diverse audiences.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Domain familiarity with marketplace trust/quality, search/ranking/relevance, or content/feedback systems is strongly preferred.

explicitly_excluded: Candidates located in: Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, Rhode Island

scope.surface: Guest-facing trip quality merchandising experience — AI-powered features (listing summaries, quality highlights, ML ranking) that help guests evaluate listing quality at booking
scope.business_model: B2C marketplace (hosts and guests), global scale
primary_metrics_inferred: Gap reduction between guest expectations and actual trip experiences
Review integrity and AI content quality
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute the product roadmap for Trip Quality Merchandising and AI, with a focus on shipping AI-powered features that improve how guests evaluate listing quality.
Define product requirements for generative AI features (e.g. listing summaries, quality highlights) and ML ranking systems, including evaluation criteria, guardrails, and iteration plans.

Partner with Data Science to assess model performance — understanding where outputs are accurate and trustworthy, and where they fall short — and translate those assessments into concrete product improvements.
Craft the product narrative that inspires teams, leadership, and the company and builds alignment on the quality merchandising strategy.
Partner with design, engineering, and customer support to deliver features that improve both guest and host experiences.
Collaborate closely with Policy, Privacy, and Legal teams on topics essential to making Airbnb one of the most trusted marketplaces in the world.
Build deep expertise in how guests evaluate listing quality through user research, marketplace data, and competitive analysis.
Make prioritization decisions that balance guest experience, platform integrity, and host impact.

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($232K-$282K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area described in detail
Role touches every booking decision — high-impact, high-visibility position
Explicit IC PM role — not a disguised people manager role
Bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned
Detailed 'A Typical Day' section gives genuine insight into the role
red_flags: Requires physical presence in San Francisco, CA — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Significant list of excluded US states may further limit candidate pool
Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates
Responsibilities section has some duplication (AI-powered features bullet appears twice), suggesting possible JD editing artifact

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + IC signal)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
location.work_auth (not explicitly stated; marked open based on no restriction mentioned)
ambiguities: The JD states candidates 'will need to be based in San Francisco, California' suggesting onsite, but does not explicitly state 100% in-office vs. hybrid schedule. Treated as onsite given the language.

The 'domain familiarity with marketplace trust/quality, search/ranking/relevance, or content/feedback systems is strongly preferred' uses 'strongly preferred' — classified as soft/nice-to-have per the preference language, though it borders on hard given the domain centrality of the role.
Responsibilities bullet about building AI-powered features appears twice in the JD — likely a copy-paste artifact; treated as one item.
